description_long: This object is a standard traffic cone with a wide, square base and a tall, conical body. The cone is primarily orange with two white reflective stripes encircling its upper section, enhancing its visibility in low-light conditions. A long, black-and-yellow striped barrier pole is attached horizontally to the top of the cone, suggesting its use for access control or demarcation. The base appears thick and sturdy, likely weighted for stability, and the cone's surface shows some signs of outdoor exposure. The tip of the cone is slightly rounded and solid, with the barrier pole affixed at the apex. No logos or text markings are visible on the exterior.
description: An orange traffic cone with white reflective stripes and a black-and-yellow barrier pole attached to its top, featuring a wide, stable base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cone's conical shape, orange color, and white stripes, with the barrier pole extending away from the viewer.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the full length of the black-and-yellow barrier pole attached to the cone's tip.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the cone and the attachment point of the barrier pole, with the stripes still visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the barrier pole extending in the other direction, mirroring view 1.
attribute_reflective_stripes: Two white reflective stripes encircle the upper section of the cone, each approximately 8 cm wide, evenly spaced.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is rounded and solid orange, with a black-and-yellow barrier pole attached horizontally at the apex.
attribute_base_design: The base is wide, square, and thick, likely weighted for stability, made of rubber or heavy plastic, with a flat bottom and no visible patterns.
attribute_presence_of_logos_or_markings: No logos or markings are visible.
attribute_condition: Lightly used, with minor signs of outdoor exposure but no significant damage or dirt.
